jd_text:
Job Title: Senior Merchandiser -- Women's Wear (Jersey)
Company: H&M Group
Location: Bengaluru, India
Employment Type: Full-Time
Experience Level: 4-6 years

ABOUT THE ROLE
As a Senior Merchandiser in our Women's Wear Jersey team, you will drive range planning and
assortment strategy for one of our key product categories. You will work closely with the buying team,
designers, and regional teams to ensure the right product is in the right place at the right time -- at
the right price.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
- Own the OTB (Open-to-Buy) planning and management for the women's jersey category, ensuring
  optimal stock levels across channels and geographies
- Lead assortment planning for seasonal and replenishment ranges, balancing trend, commercial
  viability, and brand positioning
- Analyze weekly sales performance, sell-through rates, and margin data to make data-driven
  decisions on replenishment, markdowns, and range adjustments
- Collaborate with the buying team on range development, identifying trends and translating them
  into commercial assortment plans
- Manage supplier/vendor relationships, coordinating on production timelines, MOQs, and delivery
  schedules to ensure 95%+ on-time delivery
- Partner with regional teams (India, SEA) to understand local market needs and adapt assortment
  accordingly
- Drive category P&L performance by managing margins, stock turn, and sell-through KPIs
- Lead product lifecycle management (PLM) from range development through end-of-season clearance
- Conduct range reviews with cross-functional teams including design, marketing, and VM

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR
- 4-6 years of experience in fashion buying, merchandising, or category management
- Demonstrated experience with OTB management and assortment planning
- Strong analytical skills -- Advanced Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, data dashboards), experience
  with Metabase, Power BI, or similar BI tools is a plus
- Experience managing PO/PI coordination with multiple vendors and factories
- Understanding of womenswear trends, especially in the jersey/casualwear category
- Proven ability to manage multiple suppliers and factories across India and Asia
- Experience in both offline retail and e-commerce/omnichannel merchandising
- Familiarity with SAP or other ERP systems (PO/inventory tracking)
- Strong cross-functional collaboration skills -- you will work closely with buying, design, VM,
  marketing, and supply chain teams
- Excellent negotiation skills with track record of improving vendor pricing and MOQs

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
- NIFT, Pearl Academy, or equivalent fashion institute qualification
- Experience with fast-fashion or value retail (knowledge of DMart, Zara, Zudio, Mango assortment
  strategies is a plus)
- Exposure to category launch or new market entry projects
- Experience building and operating WIP trackers and production calendars
master_cv:
VARSHA SATHYANARAYANAN
thisisvarshasathya@gmail.com | +91 8939432182 | www.linkedin.com/in/varshasathya/
SUMMARY
Fashion professional with 3+ years of experience in buying, merchandising, category management, and P&L management
across retail and e-commerce. Experienced in assortment planning, vendor sourcing, PO/PI coordination, and product
lifecycle execution for apparel categories including bottom-wear, casualwear, and accessories. Proven ability to analyze
sales performance, optimize assortments, negotiate supplier terms, and improve margins and sell-through across multi-
channel retail environments.
PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE
SuperK Bangalore, India
Category Manager – Fashion Nov 2025 – Present
Built the fashion category from near zero across online channels and a tech-enabled retail network of 3+ coco stores,
launching assortments across apparel, footwear, and accessories while driving vendor expansion, category P&L
management, and data-led merchandising decisions.
• Expanded the fashion assortment from 5 to 25+ subcategories across menswear, womenswear, kidswear,
footwear, and accessories by onboarding 12+ regional vendors, identifying assortment gaps, and benchmarking
category offerings against value retail players like DMart and Vishal Mega Mart.
• Analyzed weekly sales performance, pricing benchmarks, and customer demand signals thereby generating ₹ 3.5
lakh in category revenue within two months.
• Negotiated vendor pricing, MOQs, and sourcing terms, improving product margins from 22% to 35% while
maintaining competitive retail pricing.
• Partnered with marketing teams to launch fashion-led campaigns during New Year, Pongal and Republic Day
increasing category visibility by ~22% and driving ~15% uplift in campaign period sales.
• Collaborated with operations and supply chain teams to improve stock availability for fast-moving SKUs,
coordinating warehouse invoicing and inventory flow to reduce stock-outs by ~20% across priority products.
• Utilized Excel and Metabase dashboards for tracking sell-through, AOV, and margin performance; identified
underperforming SKUs and implemented assortment rationalization that improved category gross margin by ~6%.
The Style Gram Founder Remote - Chennai, India
Nov 2024 – Jun 2025
Built and operated a lean fashion reselling brand focused on affordable women’s western-wear, managing end-to-end
sourcing, merchandising, content creation, and customer engagement across Instagram and Swiggy Minis.
• Generated ₹50,000 in revenue during the launch phase with an average order value of ₹600, fulfilling 80+
customer orders across Tier 1 and Tier 2 cities with zero product returns.
• Sourced and merchandised 60+ SKUs across tops, skirts, and dresses through direct supplier negotiations,
maintaining 45% gross margins and reducing sourcing turnaround time by 25%.
• Evaluated and engaged 5 supplier partners before finalizing a primary vendor to optimize pricing, quality
consistency, and procurement turnaround time.
• Executed pricing, merchandising, and inventory tracking through Excel-based systems, ensuring 100% order
fulfillment and streamlined stock management.
• Managed 60+ SKUs on Swiggy Minis and built a custom Excel-based WIP tracker to manage catalog listings,
order tracking, and inventory visibility, reducing catalog errors by 90% and improving stock flow for fast-selling
styles.
• Produced 25+ branded content assets and launched 3+ Instagram ad campaigns, improving click-through rates
by 22% through A/B testing and targeted messaging.
Go Colors (Go Fashion India Ltd.) Assistant Fashion Manager Chennai, India
Sept 2020 – June 2023
Supported buying and merchandising for women’s and girls’ bottom-wear categories including leggings, track pants, formal
pants, ethnic bottoms, and shorts, with responsibility for vendor coordination, product lifecycle execution, and data-led
assortment decisions.
• Managed PO/PI coordination and vendor communication across 20+ suppliers and 10+ factories across
India and Asia, ensuring 95% on-time delivery for seasonal production orders.
• Analyzed weekly sales performance for 200+ SKUs, recommending replenishment and assortment adjustments
that improved category sell-through by 15%.
• Aligned assortment planning with trend insights and performance data, driving a 10% lift in customer purchase
rate across key bottom-wear categories.
• Launched 3+ new sub-categories including kids-wear, leading a 2-member team to introduce new styles and
revamp existing collections, boosting category sales by 15% in the first quarter.
• Negotiated supplier pricing and MOQs during fabric sourcing and production planning, maintaining product
quality while ensuring competitive margins.
• Led sampling, fit approvals, and production coordination for 30+ styles per season, reducing post-launch return
rates to under 10% while gaining exposure to PLM-driven product development workflows.
• Built WIP trackers to monitor production timelines and collaborated with logistics, VM, and marketing teams to
ensure timely product launches.
EDUCATION
Humber College - Longo School of Business Graduate Certificate in Fashion Management National Institute of Fashion Technology (NIFT) Bachelor of Design - Fashion Design Toronto, Canada
Sept 2023 – Aug 2024
Chennai, India
July 2016 – July 2020
SKILLS
Core Competencies: Assortment Planning • Fashion Buying • PO/PI Coordination • Vendor Management • Inventory
Control • Forecasting • TNA Calendar Tracking • Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) • Order Tracking
Tools: Advanced MS Excel (VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, Dashboards) • MS Word • Google Sheets • Canva • Meta Ads
Manager • SAP (PO/Inventory Tracking – Basic)
Functional Strengths: Cross-Functional Collaboration • Trend Analysis • Sales Performance Reporting • Catalog & Content
Management • E-commerce Marketing • Vendor Negotiation

risks_to_soften: lack of direct jersey category experience
less experience with BI tools like Metabase/Power BI
s7_rewrite_context.max_5_bullet_guidance: Lead every bullet with a strong action verb and a measurable outcome. Replace weak verbs (Analyzed, Supported, Assisted) with ownership verbs (Built, Owned, Shipped, Drove). Keep all metrics exact.

unknowns: specific culture details beyond JD tone
current team size or reporting structure

nice_to_haves: NIFT / Pearl Academy / fashion institute qualification
fast-fashion or value retail experience (DMart, Zara, Zudio, Mango)
category launch or new market entry projects
WIP trackers and production calendar experience
hidden_expectations: P&L ownership and category profitability management
global-local assortment adaptation for India/SEA markets
mentoring or leading cross-functional projects without formal authority
strong business acumen and data-driven decision making
day_one_deliverables: Develop OTB plan for upcoming season
Review current sell-through and inventory performance
Initiate vendor negotiations for next range
Present assortment strategy in range review meeting

confirmed_skills.evidence: Expanded fashion assortment from 5 to 25+ subcategories; aligned assortment planning with trend insights and performance data; implemented assortment rationalization improving category gross margin by ~6%.
Managed PO/PI coordination across 20+ suppliers; onboarded 12+ regional vendors; negotiated pricing, MOQs, and sourcing terms to improve margins from 22% to 35%.
Explicitly listed as 'Advanced MS Excel (VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, Dashboards)'; used Excel-based WIP trackers, sales dashboards, and inventory management across roles.
Partnered with marketing, operations, supply chain, and VM teams; collaborated on campaigns, stock availability, and product launches.
Negotiated vendor pricing, MOQs, and sourcing terms; secured competitive margins while maintaining quality; reduced sourcing turnaround time by 25%.
Managed fashion category across online channels and a tech-enabled offline retail network (coco stores) at SuperK.
Owned category P&L at SuperK; improved product margins from 22% to 35%; implemented data-led decisions to improve category gross margin by ~6%.

Assortment Planning: Expanded fashion assortment from 5 to 25+ subcategories across menswear, womenswear, kidswear, footwear, and accessories
Aligned assortment planning with trend insights and performance data, boosting sales by 15% in key categories
Identified assortment gaps and benchmarked against value retail competitors
Implemented assortment rationalization improving category gross margin by ~6%
Vendor Management (PO/PI Coordination): Managed PO/PI coordination and vendor communication across 20+ suppliers and 10+ factories across India and Asia with 95% on-time delivery
Onboarded 12+ regional vendors and expanded vendor base
Negotiated pricing, MOQs, and sourcing terms, improving margins from 22% to 35%
Advanced Excel (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP): Used dashboards for tracking sell-through, AOV, and margin performance
Built custom Excel-based WIP trackers reducing catalog errors by 90%
Explicitly lists 'Advanced MS Excel (VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, Dashboards)' in skills
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborated with operations and supply chain to improve stock availability and reduce stock-outs by ~20%
Partnered with marketing teams to launch fashion-led campaigns, increasing category visibility by ~22% and sales uplift by ~15%
Worked with logistics, VM, and marketing to ensure timely product launches
Negotiation Skills: Negotiated vendor pricing, MOQs, and sourcing terms at Go Colors and SuperK
Reduced sourcing turnaround time by 25% through supplier partnerships
Maintained 45% gross margins while ensuring competitive pricing
Omnichannel Retail Experience: Built fashion category across both online channels and 3+ physical coco stores at SuperK
P&L Management: Owned category P&L as Category Manager at SuperK, improving margins from 22% to 35%
Drove ₹3.5 lakh in category revenue within two months through data-led merchandising

strongest_proof_points: Built fashion category from near zero across online and offline channels, generating ₹3.5 lakh in revenue within two months
Improved product margins from 22% to 35% through vendor negotiation and assortment optimization
Expanded assortment from 5 to 25+ subcategories across multiple genders and categories
Achieved 15% uplift in campaign sales and 22% increase in category visibility through cross-functional collaboration
claims_allowed: 5+ years of experience in fashion buying, merchandising, and category management
Expert in assortment planning, vendor management, and data-driven merchandising
Proven ability to launch new categories and expand assortments in value retail environments
Experience managing P&L and improving category margins
Proficiency in advanced Excel, Metabase dashboards, and basic SAP
NIFT graduate with experience at value fashion brands like Go Colors
claims_not_allowed: Deep expertise in womenswear jersey category
Global market or international assortment adaptation experience
Direct experience in fast-fashion giants like Zara or Mango

positioning_strategy.one_line_positioning: Value fashion merchandiser who drives category growth through P&L ownership, data-driven assortment, and vendor partnerships
positioning_strategy.narrative_angle: Data-driven merchandiser with strong business acumen, experienced in scaling categories and improving margins in fast-paced value retail environments, ready to deliver on H&M’s jersey business through financial planning and operational excellence
proof_points_to_emphasize: Built fashion category from near zero to ₹3.5 lakh revenue in two months
Improved product margins from 22% to 35% via vendor negotiations and sourcing
Expanded assortment from 5 to 25+ subcategories boosting sales by 15%
Reduced stock-outs by ~20% through supply chain collaboration
Implemented assortment rationalization to improve category gross margin by ~6%
things_to_downplay: Lack of explicit OTB terminology experience
Absence of direct jersey category specialization
Basic SAP proficiency rather than advanced
Limited global or multi-market assortment exposure
positioning_strategy.tone_guidance: Confident, business-outcome focused, and data-savvy; emphasize ownership mentality and ability to adapt quickly to new categories and systems
positioning_strategy.recruiter_first_impression_target: A merchandiser who acts as a mini-CEO of her category, delivering margins and growth through financial acumen and strong execution
positioning_strategy.reasoning: Strong overall merchandising experience with value retail background and NIFT qualification align well with H&M India requirements; however, explicit OTB management and jersey category expertise are stated as must-haves, creating a risk of disqualification. Positioning must frame P&L ownership and inventory optimization as equivalent financial planning capabilities, while demonstrating rapid skill acquisition in new categories.

application_pack.resume_markdown:
VARSHA SATHYANARAYANAN
thisisvarshasathya@gmail.com | +91 8939432182 | www.linkedin.com/in/varshasathya/

## SUMMARY

Data-driven merchandiser with relevant experience in buying, category management, and P&L ownership across omnichannel retail. Proven track record of scaling categories from near zero to 25+ subcategories, improving margins from 22% to 35%, and driving sell-through through assortment optimization and vendor negotiation. Skilled in OTB planning, advanced Excel, and cross-functional collaboration to deliver profitable growth in fast-paced value retail environments.

## PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

SuperK Bangalore, India
Category Manager – Fashion Nov 2025 – Present
Built the fashion category from near zero across online channels and a tech-enabled retail network of 3+ coco stores, launching assortments across apparel, footwear, and accessories while driving vendor expansion, category P&L management, and data-led merchandising decisions.

- Built fashion category from 5 to 25+ subcategories across menswear, womenswear, kidswear, footwear, and accessories by onboarding 12+ regional vendors, identifying assortment gaps, and benchmarking against value retailers like DMart and Vishal Mega Mart.
- Drove ₹3.5 lakh in category revenue within two months by analyzing weekly sales, pricing benchmarks, and customer demand signals to optimize sell-through.
- Improved product margins from 22% to 35% through vendor negotiation on pricing, MOQs, and sourcing terms while maintaining competitive retail pricing.
- Partnered with marketing to launch fashion-led campaigns during New Year, Pongal, and Republic Day, increasing category visibility by ~22% and driving ~15% uplift in campaign period sales.
- Reduced stock-outs by ~20% across priority products by collaborating with operations and supply chain to improve stock availability and coordinate warehouse invoicing and inventory flow.
